name: Notion
one_liner: Connected workspace for notes, docs, wikis, and databases; its REST API lets agents read and write pages, databases, blocks, comments, and files.
access_notes: Anyone with a free Notion account can create an integration in the developer portal and instantly get a working token (internal integration token or personal access token) with no payment or human approval. OAuth 2.0 is available for public integrations. Note: on Business and Enterprise plans, PAT creation is restricted by default and must be enabled by a workspace owner, but the free/self-serve path (internal integration secret) still yields working credentials without payment.
api_notes: REST API (JSON, Bearer auth, versioned via Notion-Version header). Covers pages, databases (query/create/update schema), blocks (retrieve/append/update children), users, comments, search, file uploads, and data-source/view operations. Roughly 20-40 documented endpoints — solid CRUD coverage over core objects but not a sprawling surface. No GraphQL.
mcp_notes: Notion (vendor) ships an official MCP server: open-source local version at github.com/makenotion/notion-mcp-server plus a hosted/remote 'Notion MCP' server documented at developers.notion.com/guides/mcp. Notion is prioritizing the hosted remote server and may sunset the local repo.
main_blocker: None significant for self-serve; only caveat is PAT creation being disabled by default on Business/Enterprise plans (internal integration tokens and OAuth remain available).

evidence.claim: Notion offers a REST API with internal integration tokens, OAuth 2.0 public connections, and personal access tokens; capabilities span pages, databases, views, file uploads, comments, users, search, and webhooks.
PATs authenticate as yourself with no OAuth flow required; on Business and Enterprise plans PAT creation is restricted by default and must be enabled by a workspace owner.
Notion ships an official MCP server (makenotion/notion-mcp-server) and a hosted remote Notion MCP.
Official Notion MCP documentation and hosted server overview.

open_questions: Exact count of REST endpoints could push breadth toward NARROW/MODERATE boundary; whether any API rate/feature tier depends on paid workspace plan beyond the PAT restriction.
